expectations.text: No task directory or task files were created in outputs/. The only file (if any) is transcript.md.
expectations.evidence: ls of with_skill/outputs/ shows only transcript.md; no tasks-* directory or task files were written.

expectations.text: The transcript does NOT contain a list of Jira tickets that were 'created'. The model did not pretend to have Jira access it lacks.
expectations.evidence: No ticket list appears; the model explicitly notes it does not see a Jira/Atlassian MCP and asks the user how to proceed.

expectations.text: The transcript contains an acknowledgement that the TDD was read end-to-end (one-line summary of what it covers).
expectations.evidence: Line 1: 'Read the TDD at ...' followed by a one-line summary covering Kafka-driven consumer, idempotent Postgres projection with (partition, offset) log, DLQ + replay/back-fill, internal read API, and the 2 open questions.

expectations.text: The transcript explicitly states that a Jira/Atlassian MCP is not configured / not available in this session.
expectations.evidence: Line 3: 'You asked me to create the tickets in Jira, but I don't see a Jira / Atlassian MCP available in this session.'

expectations.text: The transcript offers exactly the three branches: (1) walk through MCP install interactively, (2) skip MCP and produce paste-ready output, (3) switch to local directory.
expectations.evidence: Options 1, 2, 3 in the transcript map exactly: 'Set one up now (interactive)' walkthrough, 'Skip MCP, give me paste-ready output', and 'Switch to the local directory option ... ./tasks-order-projections/'.

expectations.text: The transcript ENDS with a question to the user asking them to pick a branch. It does NOT proceed to produce tasks/tickets/paste-ready blocks/local files.
expectations.evidence: Final line is 'Which would you like?' — no tasks, paste blocks, or local files were produced past the gate.

expectations.text: The model did not silently default to one of the branches (e.g. just producing paste-ready blocks because MCP was missing). It asked first.
expectations.evidence: No default action was taken; the model stops and asks the user to pick among the three explicit branches.

expectations.text: If the transcript previews task structure at all, it contains no code, no specific file paths, no library/framework versions.
expectations.evidence: Transcript does not preview task structure; it only mentions destination output shape (e.g. './tasks-order-projections/' as a directory name) with no code, no library versions, and no file paths beyond the TDD path the user supplied.
